methodologies.trigger_scenario: The user wants to test a product idea with the smallest useful experiment.
methodologies.core_goal: define the smallest product or experiment that can test the riskiest assumption

steps.name: Identify the riskiest assumption
steps.instruction: Separate the user's idea into assumptions about users, value, acquisition, payment, retention, and feasibility. Pick the assumption that can invalidate the idea fastest.

steps.name: Define the smallest experiment
steps.instruction: Design the minimum product, landing page, concierge service, prototype, manual workflow, or test that can validate the assumption.

steps.name: Set the learning metric
steps.instruction: Choose one clear success metric and one decision threshold before the experiment starts.

steps.name: Decide next action
steps.instruction: Recommend persevere, iterate, or pivot based on the expected evidence.

methodologies.expected_output: A concise MVP experiment plan with assumption, prototype scope, metric, threshold, timeline, risk, and next action.
methodologies.source_basis: Core Lean Startup methodology commonly associated with MVP and validated learning.
methodologies.risk_notes: Exact chapter references should be confirmed during live research.

methodologies.trigger_scenario: The user wants to run an iterative product learning cycle.
methodologies.core_goal: turn an idea into a measurable experiment and convert results into a decision

steps.name: Build
steps.instruction: Translate the hypothesis into the smallest build action that can generate real user evidence.

steps.name: Measure
steps.instruction: Define actionable metrics, collection method, sample, and observation period.

steps.name: Learn
steps.instruction: Interpret the results against the pre-set decision rule. Summarize what has been validated or invalidated.

steps.name: Shorten the loop
steps.instruction: Suggest ways to reduce cost, time, and complexity in the next cycle.

methodologies.expected_output: A Build Measure Learn cycle plan and learning record.
methodologies.risk_notes: Keep metrics actionable and avoid vanity metrics.

methodologies.trigger_scenario: The user has product evidence and wants to decide whether to change direction.
methodologies.core_goal: diagnose whether to persevere, iterate, or make a strategic pivot

steps.name: Review evidence
steps.instruction: Summarize what the product team has learned from users, metrics, interviews, and experiments.

steps.name: Identify the failed assumption
steps.instruction: Find which assumption no longer holds, such as value, customer segment, channel, pricing, growth, or technology.

steps.name: Select pivot type
steps.instruction: Recommend the most suitable shift, such as customer segment pivot, zoom-in pivot, zoom-out pivot, channel pivot, value capture pivot, or technology pivot.

steps.name: Design the next validation
steps.instruction: Turn the pivot recommendation into a new testable hypothesis and experiment plan.

methodologies.expected_output: A pivot diagnosis report with evidence, failed assumption, recommended pivot, risks, and next validation experiment.
methodologies.source_basis: Lean Startup pivot and persevere decision logic.
methodologies.risk_notes: Do not recommend a pivot only because growth is temporarily slow.
